rulings.Case Title: KATHLEEN M BARISH et al VS. STEVEN D BROOKS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Jay Morton To Respond To Interrogatories And Demand For Production
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 3, 2012, Line 1. CROSS COMPLAINANT STEVEN BROOKS' Motion To Compel Jay Morton To Respond To Interrogatories And Demand For Production. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/KH)

rulings.Case Title: VASILIKI SERVETA VS. FINANCE AMERICA, LLC C/O MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Set Aside Judge Pro Tem Rebecca Louise Woodson'S March 29, 2012 Discovery Order For Judcial Misconduct And To Amend Complaint To Add Rebecca Louise Woodson As Doe 1; Memo Of P&A In Support; Decl Of Pltf Vasiliki Serveta In Support
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 3, 2012, line 5, PLAINTIFF VASILIKI SERVETA'S MOTION To Set Aside Judge Pro Tem Rebecca Louise Woodson'S March 29, 2012 Discovery Order For JudIcial Misconduct And To Amend Complaint To Add Rebecca Louise Woodson As Doe 1. Off calendar. The appeal of the order sought to be vacated deprives the court of jurisdiction to grant the relief sought. Moreover, if the court had jurisdiction to grant the relief, the motion would be denied because a relationship with a lender is not grounds for disqualification of a judicial officer. (Rothman, California Judicial Conduct Handbook (2007) p. 323 at n. 112). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BRET CORNELL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 3, 2012, line 6, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY, POLICE OFFICER RICHARD BODISCO, INDIVIDUALLY, POLICE OFFICER DAVID BRANDT, INDIVIDUALLY, POLICE OFFICER JESSE FARRELL, INDIVIDUALLY, POLICE SERGEANT WALLACE GIN, INDIVIDUALLY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied and the motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 10, granted as to the City only as to issue 8, off calendar as moot as to issues 2, 3 and 4 and as to issue 9 as to the City only, denied as to issues 5, 6 and 7, and as to the issues 8 and 9 as to the City employees only. As to issue 1, the alleged excessive force used against Plaintiff was inherent in the detention itself. As to issues 5, 6 and 7, there are triable issues whether there was reasonable suspicion to detain, reasonable circumstances to draw a gun, and probable cause to arrest. As to issues 8 and 9, the City cannot interfere with its own economic relationship with Plaintiff, but there are triable issues whether Defendants Bodisco, Brandt, Farrell, and Gin so interfered and did so by engaging in wrongful conduct. As to issue 10, Mr. Gascon has satisfied his burden of production and plaintiff has not presentd any evidence showing that Mr. Gascon had personal involvement in the incident. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WENDY WESTMAN VS. ROBERT SHAPIRO DR. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Plaintiff'S Medical And Psychiatric Records And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 3, 2012, Line 3. DEFENDANT BRADLEY SHAPIRO, M.D.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Plaintiff'S Medical And Psychiatric Records And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, with monetary sanctions awarded against plaintiff Wendy Westman in the amount of $595. The Motion is granted on the grounds set forth in the moving papers and the motion is not opposed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com, referencing the case name and number in the subject line, with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/KH)
